title: fix(quadlet): parse comment lines with kv-pairs
body:
#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

None

```release-note
Fix loop in quadlet unitfile parser
```

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **NOT APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *jwhb* **Once this PR has been reviewed and has the lgtm label**, please assign [luap99](https://github.com/luap99) for approval. For more information see [the Kubernetes Code Review Process](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process). The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman).
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - **[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/v4.9-rhel/OWNERS)** Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
A friendly reminder that this PR had no activity for 30 days.
@jwhb are you still working on this?

I have not tested as there is no example unit provided here but there was a recent fix on main https://github.com/containers/podman/commit/64e94efb95e099db93ee8a28735549268490190f that sounds similar so please check if this is fixed
This affects only 4.9, in podman 5.x the behavior is fixed due to a new implementation of the parser.

Do you have the commit that fixed this?

In order to get anything fixed in a old RHEL branch you must file a RHEL jira against podman (or contact Red Hat support and let them file the issue) and request a backport there. Without approved backports by the RHEL process we cannot accept any code change in the RHEL branch.

I think the bug was mitigated by https://github.com/containers/podman/commit/d47979761f205601dc031ef5c21d9740d2b7ec91 (podman 5.0) and fixed for good in https://github.com/containers/podman/commit/64e94efb95e099db93ee8a28735549268490190f (currently only main).

I am not affected by this bug as a Red Hat customer.
